cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. Lily is interested in nature and American history. I need to figure out which option combines both of those elements. First, looking at the options: A) The Grand Canyon and Rodeo Drive. The Grand Canyon is mentioned as a place for hikers and nature lovers, so that's good for the nature part. Rodeo Drive is in Los Angeles and is about shopping, maybe seeing stars. But Rodeo Drive doesn't seem related to history or nature. So maybe this isn't the best choice. B) California and Los Angeles. California is a state, and Los Angeles is a city there. The text mentions Hollywood Walk of Fame and Rodeo Drive in LA. Again, more about entertainment and shopping than history or nature. So B might not be right. C) White House and Arizona. The White House is in Washington, D.C., which is noted for historical places like the Lincoln Memorial. Arizona has the Grand Canyon. The White House is definitely American history, and the Grand Canyon is nature. So this combines both interests. D) C and The Grand Canyon. Wait, option C is White House and Arizona. If D is combining C (which includes White House) with The Grand Canyon, then that would be White House (history) and Grand Canyon (nature). But Arizona is already part of option C, and the Grand Canyon is in Arizona. So maybe D is redundant? Let me check the options again. Wait, the choices are (A) The Grand Canyon and Rodeo Drive; (B) California and Los Angeles; (C) White House and Arizona; (D) C and The Grand Canyon. So option D is combining option C (White House and Arizona) with The Grand Canyon. But Arizona already includes the Grand Canyon. So D would be White House (history) and Grand Canyon (nature) since Arizona is the location of the Grand Canyon. So option C is White House
